body_txt: Ghosts sometimes develop certain powers, quirks, or a theme based on how they died. For example, somebody who died in a fire may have Playing with Fire abilities, which might include Flaming Hair or their body being partly or wholly Wreathed in Flames . Someone who suffered an icy death might be An Ice Person whose body exudes a Ghostly Chill or has Ice Crystals formed on it. A person who drowned may have Making a Splash powers and be covered in barnacles or other marine life, even if they perished in a lake or river. An individual who was electrocuted might end up as an electromagnetic ghost with Shock and Awe and/or Technopath abilities. A person who starved to death may have Horror Hunger -themed powers. Somebody who was poisoned might end up as a Poisonous Person . Someone who died of an illness may become a Plaguemaster . Sub-Trope of Our Ghosts Are Different . Compare to the above-mentioned Electromagnetic Ghosts and Ghostly Chill , both of which can overlap with this. Also compare to Death-Activated Superpower , Elemental Powers (if the ghost's abilities relate to a certain element), and Personality Powers (and its Sub-Trope Elemental Personalities ), all of which may overlap with this. See also Jacob Marley Apparel , when a person's cause of death may affect the clothes and injuries that they wear or display as a ghost and might overlap with this — it's essentially a sister trope focusing on Death-Based Ghost Appearance, as it were.
